summary: Heavily obfuscated shim warrants manual review
details: The package ships a heavily obfuscated file at shim/index.js using hex-name variable mangling (e.g., _0x391f3f, _0x3eff0a, _0x534564) consistent with a javascript-obfuscator string-array transform. No traced behavior was confirmed identifying exfiltration, install-time fetch-and-execute, silent-relay, or other concrete installer harm. Obfuscation alone is not malicious, but the lack of source transparency in a shim module — together with the package name shape — is a meaningful concern that warrants human review before trusting the package in a build.

cwes.description: The product contains code that appears to be malicious in nature.
cwes.name: Embedded Malicious Code
